audit_items.proof_trace: All six dynamic allocations in main.c (kzalloc for abm/alink/prio_map, bitmap_zalloc for threshold_undef, two kvcalloc for thresholds/actions) are properly freed via the classic Linux kernel staircase-goto error pattern in nfp_abm_init and on success by nfp_abm_clean/nfp_abm_vnic_free. Every error branch frees exactly the allocations made before that point, and the success-path cleanup functions (nfp_abm_clean, nfp_abm_vnic_free) free all owned memory including repr/port objects managed by helper functions. The dscp_map list items are allocated in cls.c (not main.c) and are out of scope for this property.
